title: [RELAX] Fix rotary embedding buffer size calculation
body:
- Change head_dim//2 to rotary_dim//2 in LongRope scaling
- Fixes buffer size when rotary_dim differs from head_dim when partial_rotary_factor < 1.0

files_modified.filename: tests (new/updated) - likely under tests/python/relax or tests/python/relax/frontend/nn/llm

step_plan.summary: Fix Relax LongRoPE rotary embedding scaling to size the ext_factors buffer by rotary_dim//2 (not head_dim//2), ensuring correct buffer contracts when partial rotary embeddings are used (rotary_dim != head_dim). Also aligns/document assumptions in LongRoPE frequency computation to prevent future mismatches.

steps.operation: Fix LongRoPE ext_factors buffer shape to use rotary_dim instead of head_dim in the LongRoPE fused TIR primfunc signature
steps.reason: In the LongRoPE path, ext_factors is indexed by the rotary frequency dimension (d_range == rotary_dim), so the buffer contract must be sized as rotary_dim//2. Using head_dim//2 breaks partial rotary (partial_rotary_factor < 1.0) where rotary_dim != head_dim, leading to shape mismatches and potential incorrect memory access assumptions.
steps.side_effects: Changes the generated TIR function signature/buffer map for LongRoPE scaling; any caller that constructs/provides rope_ext_factors must match the new expected length (rotary_dim//2). May require updating downstream code or tests that hardcode ext_factors size to head_dim//2.

steps.operation: Audit and document/align ext_factors indexing assumptions in LongRoPE frequency computation to ensure consistency with rotary_dim-sized ext_factors
steps.reason: rope_freq_longrope uses ext_factors[d % (d_range // 2)] with d_range passed as rotary_dim. While the math is likely correct, adding an explicit expectation (and ensuring no hidden dependence on head_dim) prevents regressions and clarifies that ext_factors length must be rotary_dim//2.
steps.side_effects: No intended runtime behavior change beyond clarifying/enforcing invariants; if additional checks/assertions are added, they could fail earlier for incorrectly sized ext_factors and may require test updates.
